recommended_path.summary: Start with a narrow HVAC-specific service tied to revenue, validate with local owner conversations and paid pilots, then productize the repeated workflow into a micro-SaaS.
reasoning: Warm access to HVAC owners is a stronger early distribution advantage than building an audience from scratch.
Services create fast learning loops and earlier cash flow.
A narrow operational wedge is easier to sell and later convert into software than a broad AI product.

top_opportunity.name: Missed-call and lead follow-up automation
why: Pain is common and easy for owners to understand.
Value is tied directly to recovered revenue.
Can be delivered manually or with lightweight tooling before full productization.
Supports a natural evolution into a micro-SaaS.

other_opportunities: Technician note summarization and quote drafting

traits: Small office team handling phones and scheduling
Missed after-hours leads or slow follow-up
Not advanced enough to build internal tooling
Able to pay for clear ROI

risks: Building software before validating the workflow
Focusing on low-value time savings instead of revenue impact
first_30_days: Interview 10 HVAC owners through warm intros
Refine one narrow offer around missed-call and lead follow-up recovery
Track response time, recovered leads, and booked jobs
Write one case study and identify the first reusable software module
